results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO CREATE A TRACKING SYSTEM THAT COMBINES HEAT AND AIR POLLUTION EFFECTS AT THE NEAR SURFACE OR LOCALIZED MICROCLIMATIC CONDITIONS TO MEASURE A COMFORT INDEX VALUE WITHIN VULNERABLE COMMUNITIES. ACTIVITIES:TO CREATE A COMFORT INDEX, THE PROJECT WILL 1) DEPLOY DRONE MAPPING OF THE BUILT ENVIRONMENT IN SELECTED NEIGHBORHOODS MOST AT RISK FROM AIR POLLUTANTS AND HIGH TEMPERATURES, 2) USE MODELING TOOLS TO SIMULATE THE URBAN MICRO-CLIMATE, INCLUDING HEAT AND AIR POLLUTION, FOR REAL-TIME SIMULATION OF CONDITIONS THAT INDIVIDUALS EXPERIENCE, AND 3) ENGAGE COMMUNITY MEMBERS TO MONITOR POLLUTION DATA USING SMALL POLLUTION MONITORING DEVICES THAT TRAVEL THROUGH THE SELECTED NEIGHBORHOODS.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE A FINAL REPORT AS WELL AS A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF THE NEAR SURFACE IMPACT OF INCREASED TEMPERATURE AND AIR POLLUTION ON THE HEALTH OF THE CITY OF BIRMINGHAM, ALABAMA. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE THE COMMUNITY MEMBERS OF BIRMINGHAM, URBAN PLANNERS AND DESIGNERS, AND STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT.
